title: acacia deltoidea subsp. deltoidea
intro: Acacia deltoidea subsp. deltoidea, also known as the "Khadira" or "Black Acacia", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acacia deltoidea subsp. deltoidea include reducing fever, treating wounds, and managing diabetes, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, and its bark and leaves are also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
